application.doc: Rotate the secret for a specified API protection client

param.name: client_id
param.doc: The system-assigned API protection client ID value

method.name: POST
method.id: Rotate the secret for a specified API protection client
method.doc: To rotate the secret of an existing API protection client, issue an HTTP POST command on the client secrets resource URI specifying the ID of the API protection client.
Note: This API is only available for clients with hash secrets enabled.
Note: For the changes to take effect, they must be deployed as described in the Configuration changes commit process.

request.doc: To determine the client_id value of the client to be updated, issue an HTTP GET command on the clients resource URI: https://{appliance_hostname}/iam/access/v8/clients/.

param.name: Accept:application/json
param.doc: Required for requests to the service.

param.name: name
param.doc: A meaningful name to identify the new API protection client secret.

param.name: clientSecret
param.doc: A string that identifies the new client secret. The client secret mechanism is a means of authorizing a client. Applications requesting an access token must know the client secret in order to gain the token. If omitted, the value is set to null and the client is considered a public client. If secret hashing is enabled use ******** to preserve the existing secret.

param.name: keepPreviousPrimary
param.doc: Whether or not to keep the existing primary client secret as active. Has the effect of having multiple client secrets. If omitted, the value is set to false.

param.name: expiration
param.doc: The milliseconds sinch epoch at which time the previous primary stored secret will remain active. Only valid if keepPreviousPrimary is set to true. If not set or set to 0 the secret will not expire.

param.name: clientSecret
param.doc: A string that identifies the new client secret. The client secret mechanism is a means of authorizing a client. Applications requesting an access token must know the client secret in order to gain the token. If secret hashing is enabled the secret cannot be retrieved after this response.

response.doc: Bad Request. There is a problem with the request. The JSON result describes the problem.
Not Found. The requested service does not exist. The JSON message indicates which resource.
Conflict. The JSON result indicates the problem.
An internal error occurred. The JSON message indicates the problem.
